per_question.question_text: Can you tell me about the difference between a LEFT JOIN and an INNER JOIN in SQL and when you might use one over the other?
per_question.answer_text: An inner join returns only the records that are matching values in both tables. If there is no match, That raw is X Excluded A left join returns all records from the left table. and matching records from the right table. If there is no match, the right side column will contain null's value. In my SQL project, I frequently use left join. Fine When checking missing relationship Keep In inner joint When analyzing only matching business data

per_question.question_text: Tell me about a time you had to present data to someone who wasn't technical. How did you make sure your insights were clear and actionable for them?
per_question.answer_text:
Initial answer:
During my internship I presented dashboard inside to manage a Uber not familiar with SQL or analytics. Instead of discussing queries or calculation I focused on business outcomes. For example rather that saying fuel consumption variance increase by 8% I explained these This route is consuming more fuel than expected, which could increase operating cost Optimizing.

Instead of discussing queries or calculation, I focused on business outcomes. For example, rather than saying fuel consumption varies increased by 8%, I explain. This route is consuming more fuel than expected. which could increase operating cost First, optimizing this route may reduce expenses. I also used simple charts, KPI cards, and color indicators, so the information was easy to understand.

per_question.question_text: Could you explain what data cleaning is and give me a couple of examples of common data quality issues you've encountered?
per_question.answer_text: Data cleaning is the process of preparing raw data before analysis so that the results are accurate and reliable. Some common data quality issues include missing values, duplicate records, incorrect formatting, inconsistent spelling. Extra spaces invalidates outliers. To solve this problem, I use function like like trim, clean. If error, remove duplicate text. Text to column and conditional formatting. In SQL I used update statement case expressions. Coalescence and filtering techniques. Thanks I always verify the clean data before beginning any analysis.
per_question.fix: practice using advanced data cleaning techniques in SQL and Excel
